title: baphia polygalacea
intro: Baphia polygalacea, also known as the "wild marigold" or "mata leão," is a tropical plant species native to the savannas and grasslands of Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Baphia polygalacea include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Baphia polygalacea is often used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
